item.title: Predicting ESG risks with Pytorch, Google Trends and Amazon SageMaker
item.description: Predicting ESG risks with Pytorch, Google Trends and Amazon SageMaker 0. README I conducted this project as part of the Machine Learning Engineer Nanodegree at Udacity in August 2020. Python libraries It relies on the following libraries or services Python (3.6) Pytorch (1.4.0) Amazon Sagemaker Pytrends (4.7.3, https://pypi.org/project/pytrends/) Yahooquery (2.2.6, https://pypi.org/project/yahooquery/) Files There are several files Running 1_data_collection.ipynb takes approximately 17 hours due to Google’s rate limit and a timeout function which avoids server errors.

item.title: A comprehensive guide to Causal Forest - How it works and how to apply it
item.description: Disclaimer This guide aims to be a great resource for practitioners of causal forest and wants to empower researchers with this awesome method. I found myself diving into its R source code, published papers on its theory and referring to its algorithm reference. I want to combine all these resources in one article, so you do not need to check every resource in-detail and dig deep. Still, you are welcome to do so and I refer everything under references.

item.title: Design Recommender Systems with Recommenderlab
item.description: Build your own recommender system with Recommenderlab I show Recommenderlab’s scope to customize recommender algorithms and discuss how to compare different recommender techniques.
